commit.message:
fix(v2twilio): skip full pipeline on ghost turns with pending questions

Bridge timeout's actionOnEmptyResult:true creates ghost turns with empty
speechResult. Previously these ran the full bridge+runtime+ScrabEngine
pipeline, causing confusing "I'm connecting you to our team" messages and
T3 fallback on INPUT_TOO_SHORT. Now when empty input arrives and a
pending follow-up question (PFUQ) or legacy pending question (PQ) exists:

1. Ghost turn early-exit guard returns a silent Gather immediately
2. Pending question turns are bumped forward to survive for the real turn
3. Bridge is also disabled when PFUQ is active (was only checking legacy PQ)
4. Turn tolerance widened to ±2 gap to absorb ghost turn counter bumps

Prevents: bridge hold messages, handleTransfer, ScrabEngine, and fallback
from firing on ghost turns. Caller's next real speech goes directly to
the consent gate as intended.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): force Secure attribute on SameSite=None session cookies per RFC 6265bis

Browsers reject Set-Cookie headers with SameSite=None that lack the Secure
attribute. The session cookie builder now automatically includes Secure when
SameSite is set to None, regardless of the explicit secure config flag. This
prevents silent cookie rejection in cross-site contexts (OAuth callbacks,
embedded iframes, third-party integrations).

commit.message:
Async FT2 decoder: sliding-window continuous decode

New "Async" checkbox enables continuous FT2 decoding independent of period
boundaries. Uses a 7.5s ring buffer and decodes every 1.5s via sliding
window — signals are decoded regardless of when they are transmitted.

- lib/ft2/ft2_async_decode.f90: new Fortran module ft2_async_mod with
  standalone ft2_async_decode() callable from C++ via QtConcurrent::run
  (same pattern as fast_decode_ for MSK144)
- mainwindow: async ring buffer (90k samples), 1.5s QTimer trigger,
  deduplication set (10s window), asyncDecodeDone() result handler
- mainwindow.ui: cbAsyncDecode checkbox
- CMakeLists.txt: add ft2_async_decode.f90 to build

feat(overlay-protocol): add Zig FFI implementing all five invariant checks

C-compatible FFI matching the Idris2 ABI: PeerType enum, OverlayDeclaration
struct, BaseState, ConformanceFlags bitfield. Functions for each invariant:
overlay_check_non_modification, overlay_check_additive, overlay_check_switchable,
overlay_check_idempotent, overlay_check_declaration. Full conformance via
overlay_check_conformance. Composition and mixed peer-type composability.

14 unit tests + 8 integration tests (scenarios: HOL-o-extension,
aggregate-library, base-modification, non-reversible activation,
mixed composability, incomplete declaration). All pass on Zig 0.15.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove push notification capabilities for free Apple Developer account

Removed push notification and time sensitive notification capabilities
to enable deployment on physical iPhone with Personal Team (free account).

Changes:
- Removed Push Notifications capability from entitlements
- Removed Time Sensitive Notifications capability
- Updated IzzyYum.entitlements to empty dict
- Registered device: EKiPhone (00008150-001A43500201401C)
- Configured automatic signing with Edwin Kirschner (Personal Team)

Personal development teams do not support:
- Push Notifications capability
- Time Sensitive Notifications capability

These require a paid Apple Developer Program membership ($99/year).

For MVP testing without paid account:
- App can still receive data via Realtime sync (WebSocket)
- All UI features work normally
- Can test shopping lists, categories, checkboxes
- Just can't test push notifications from web app

To re-enable push notifications:
1. Enroll in Apple Developer Program
2. Re-add Push Notifications capability in Xcode
3. Restore entitlements configuration

App now successfully deploys and runs on physical iPhone.

Related: #10

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

with appendices covering all 87+ games across 9 strategic domains,
coalition formation, meta-governance engine, N-player extensions,
GRPO/DPO training pipeline, and safety transfer evaluation.

New sections: game library, N-player, coalition, governance, dynamic
game creation, evaluation protocol, training pipeline, safety transfer,
discussion. New appendices: game catalog, formal specs, implementation,
extended results, reproducibility. References expanded to 27 entries
with Scholar-verified metadata. Adds automated bib cleanup script.

commit.message:
Classify ClassPropertyQualifiers → §18.4 [skip ci]

Section 18.4 ("Random variables") presents Syntax 18-1, which is the authoritative BNF for class_property with property_qualifier, explicitly showing that property_qualifier encompasses both random_qualifier (rand | randc) and class_item_qualifier (static | protected | local). The test exercises exactly this production — one member with rand (random_qualifier) and one with local (class_item_qualifier) — making §18.4 the single most relevant subclause.

commit.message:
feat(browser): add host-ready notification to browser reactor and edge bridge types to Next.js SDK

Browser reactor gains notify_ready() — the key integration point for browser
host bridges (fetch completion, WebSocket events, stream callbacks) to deliver
token readiness into the reactor event queue. The method:
- Validates the token exists and the readiness intersects registered interest
- Coalesces with existing pending events when coalesce_wakes is enabled
- Sets wake_pending atomically so the next poll picks up the events

Extracted readiness_mask() as a shared helper replacing inline Interest
flag unions. The reregister path now filters pending events to remove
stale readiness bits that no longer match the updated interest mask.

wake() rewritten to snapshot readiness events and merge them into the
pending queue atomically, fixing a race where wake_pending could be set
without any events actually being queued.

Next.js SDK (@asupersync/next) adds full edge bridge type surface:
- NextEdgeBridgeEnvironment, NextEdgeBridgeDiagnostics,
  NextEdgeBridgeRequestOptions, NextEdgeBridgeRequest,
  NextEdgeBridgeResponse, NextEdgeBridgeAdapterOptions,
  NextEdgeBridgeRuntimeError types
- createNextEdgeBridgeDiagnostics() factory function
- createNextEdgeBridgeAdapter() for constructing edge-safe bridge adapters
  that explicitly disable direct runtime execution
- createNextBridgeLogFields() broadened to accept edge diagnostics
- NEXT_EDGE_BRIDGE_ENVIRONMENTS constant

Minor fixes across other reactor backends (epoll, kqueue, macos, windows,
source) for consistency and formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: register all geofence regions in single startGeofencingAsync call

startGeofencingAsync replaces the full monitored set on every call.
The previous one-by-one loop meant only the last geofence survived.

- startMonitoringRegion: add to map first, then call updateActiveRegions
  with all active regions (not just the new one)
- add syncRegions(): atomic full-set replacement for bulk syncs
- syncMonitoring in useGeofences: build desired set, call syncRegions
  once instead of looping per-geofence
- add defensive logging to background task and registration paths
